title: A fluid testing system
abstract: A fluid testing system is envisaged which comprises a source that generates electromagnetic waves, a detector that receives the transmitted electromagnetic waves and generates analog signals corresponding to the colours represented in the electromagnetic waves and, a receptacle, having transparent walls and a fluid inlet. The receptacle is positioned to be between the source and the detector to enable the electromagnetic waves to pass through its walls and through a fluid sample present in the receptacle. The system comprises a repository that stores a pre-determined range of reference values corresponding to the values in the digital signals for fluids of various colours. An analog to digital converter present in the system cooperates with the detector to receive the analog signals and convert them into digital signals, the values of which are then compared with the reference values by a comparator to determine the colour values of the fluid sample and hence adulteration level of the fluid.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A fluid testing system, said system comprising: a source configured to generate electromagnetic waves; a detector configured to receive electromagnetic waves transmitted by the source and generate analog signals corresponding to the colours represented in the electromagnetic waves; a receptacle, having transparent walls and a fluid inlet, positioned between said source and said detector, said receptacle configured to enable said electromagnetic waves to pass through its walls and through a fluid sample filled in said receptacle; a repository configured to store a pre-determined range of reference values corresponding to the values in the digital signals for fluids of various colours; an analog to digital converter configured to cooperate with said detector to receive the analog signals and convert them into digital signals; a comparator configured to receive said digital signals and compare the values in the digital signals with said reference values to determine colour values of the fluid sample thereby determining adulteration level of fluid. 2. The system as claimed in claim 1 , which comprises an opaque housing covering the receptacle, said housing having at least one first hole for placement of said source and at least one second hole for placement of said detector. 3. The system as claimed in claim 1, wherein said source is at least one light emitting diode. 4. The system as claimed in claim 1 , wherein said detector is at least one selected from the group consisting of photodiode, optical sensor and radiation camera. 5. The system as claimed in claim 1, which includes a display configured to display the adulteration level of the fluid. 6. The system as claimed in claim 1, which includes a pump configured to pump fluid in said receptacle. 7. The system as claimed in claim 1, which is fitted between the fuel inlet nozzle and the fuel tank of a vehicle, and includes a fluid diverter connected to said receptacle to selectively divert fuel contained there within, based on the determined adulteration level, in said fuel tank and an auxiliary tank. 8. The system as claimed in claim 7, wherein said fluid diverter is a valve. 9. The system as claimed in claim 7, wherein said auxiliary tank has an opening configured to drain out fuel contained therein. 10. The system as claimed in claim 7, which includes an override mode in which said system remains in an inoperative configuration and thereby permits the flow of fuel to the fuel tank and restricts the flow of fuel to the auxiliary tank.
